# Kumiki — full reference (machine-readable)

> Per https://llmstxt.org/. This file is generated by apps/docs/scripts/build-llms-full.mjs.
> Each package gets a section with its description, first JSDoc block, and exported type names.

## @kumiki/atelier

Layer 5 preview — opinionated, styled component variants built on top of @kumiki/components. Ships under the `preview` dist-tag during the v1.0 series.

### Overview

`@kumiki/atelier` — barrel that re-exports every Atelier component as a namespace.

Prefer the per-component subpath (`@kumiki/atelier/toggle`) for tree-shake
guarantees. This barrel exists for one-shot imports.

Implementation note: matches the `export * as` namespace pattern from
`@kumiki/components` to handle generic Svelte components consistently
(sveltejs/svelte#12785).

Source: `packages/atelier/`

---

## @kumiki/cli

Kumiki CLI: copy Layer 5 Atelier components (@kumiki/atelier) into a project.

Source: `packages/tooling/cli/`

---

## @kumiki/components

Layer 4 — every Kumiki Svelte 5 component (Toggle, Dialog, Combobox, …) under a single install. Subpath per component plus a dot-namespace barrel.

### Overview

`@kumiki/components` — every Kumiki Svelte 5 component, exposed as a
dot-namespace barrel.

Prefer the subpath import for the cleanest tree-shake on older
bundlers (`import { Root } from '@kumiki/components/toggle'`); the
barrel below is for ergonomic namespaced access:

```svelte
<script>
  import { Toggle, Dialog, Combobox } from '@kumiki/components';
</script>

<Toggle.Root>Bold</Toggle.Root>
<Dialog.Root>...</Dialog.Root>
```

Implementation note: we use `export * as` namespace re-exports rather
than `export const Toggle = { Root, … }` because generic Svelte
components emit `$$IsomorphicComponent` in their `.d.ts` which can't
cross module boundaries through a const-bound object
(sveltejs/svelte#12785).

### Exports

- `Toggle`

Source: `packages/components/`

---

## @kumiki/headless

Layer 3 — Svelte 5 attachments that wire @kumiki/machines to real DOM. One subpath per component; install once, import the parts you use.

### Overview

`@kumiki/headless` — barrel that re-exports every attachment factory.

Prefer the per-component subpath (`@kumiki/headless/toggle`,
`@kumiki/headless/combobox`, …) for tree-shake guarantees. This barrel
exists to make `import * as headless from '@kumiki/headless'` work and
to keep one-shot integrations terse.

Each component is a namespace to prevent name collisions (multiple
components export `Controller`, etc).

### Exports

- `index_d_exports`
- `index_d_exports$1`
- `index_d_exports$10`
- `index_d_exports$11`
- `index_d_exports$12`
- `index_d_exports$13`
- `index_d_exports$14`
- `index_d_exports$15`
- `index_d_exports$2`
- `index_d_exports$3`
- `index_d_exports$4`
- `index_d_exports$5`
- `index_d_exports$6`
- `index_d_exports$7`
- `index_d_exports$8`
- `index_d_exports$9`

Source: `packages/headless/`

---

## @kumiki/locale

Locale data — dynamically importable per language. Initial set: en, ja, zh-Hans, zh-Hant, ko, es, fr, de, ar, he.

### Overview

`@kumiki/locale` — type-only barrel.

This entry exports the `Messages` shape and `Direction` tag. Per-language
data is **not** re-exported here — consumers import from the per-language
subpath (`@kumiki/locale/en`, `@kumiki/locale/ja`, …) so only the locale
they actually use ends up in their bundle.

@see docs/design/06-i18n.md

### Exports

- `Direction`

Source: `packages/core/locale/`

---

## @kumiki/machines

Layer 2 — pure-TS finite state machines for every Kumiki component. One subpath per component; no DOM, no Svelte.

### Overview

`@kumiki/machines` — barrel that re-exports every component machine.

Prefer the per-component subpath (`@kumiki/machines/toggle`,
`@kumiki/machines/combobox`, …) for tree-shake guarantees. This barrel
exists for one-shot cases like type indexing in tooling, and to keep
`import * as machines from '@kumiki/machines'` working.

Each component is exposed as a namespace to prevent name collisions
(e.g. multiple components have a `Context` type).

### Exports

- `index_d_exports`
- `index_d_exports$1`
- `index_d_exports$10`
- `index_d_exports$11`
- `index_d_exports$12`
- `index_d_exports$13`
- `index_d_exports$14`
- `index_d_exports$15`
- `index_d_exports$2`
- `index_d_exports$3`
- `index_d_exports$4`
- `index_d_exports$5`
- `index_d_exports$6`
- `index_d_exports$7`
- `index_d_exports$8`
- `index_d_exports$9`

Source: `packages/machines/`

---

## @kumiki/primitives

Layer 1 framework-agnostic primitives (focus-trap, dismissable, id, locale, live-region, collection, interactions, motion, portal).

### Overview

`@kumiki/primitives` — Layer 1 framework-agnostic primitives.

Each primitive is also available as a sub-path import — e.g.,
`import { uid } from '@kumiki/primitives/id'` — for surgical tree-shaking.
The barrel below is for users who want everything at once.

See `docs/design/02-architecture.md` §2.3.

### Exports

- `CollectionItem`
- `LOCALE_CONTEXT_KEY`
- `createDismissable`
- `createFocusTrap`
- `createIdScope`
- `findByTypeAhead`
- `getNextEnabledId`
- `inferDirection`
- `tabindexFor`
- `uid`

Source: `packages/core/primitives/`

---

## @kumiki/runtime

Minimal FSM runtime — pure TypeScript, framework-agnostic, with XState-compatible JSON export for visualization.

### Overview

`@kumiki/runtime` — minimal finite state machine runtime for Kumiki.

Pure TypeScript, framework-agnostic. ~1 KB gzipped target. See
`docs/design/04-state-machines.md` and `docs/design/16-decisions/0003-state-machine-base.md`.

### Exports

- `Action`
- `defineMachine`

Source: `packages/core/runtime/`

---

## @kumiki/types

Shared TypeScript types — Standard Schema v1 contract used across Form Field, Combobox `withValidation`, and any other validator surface. Zero runtime cost.

### Overview

`@kumiki/types` — shared type-only definitions used across Kumiki layers.

Zero runtime cost — every export is `type` or `interface`. Importing from
here in your own code adds nothing to the bundle. Currently exposes the
Standard Schema v1 contract that drives validation across Form Field,
Combobox `withValidation`, and any other surface that takes a validator.

@see https://standardschema.dev/

### Exports

- `StandardSchemaResult`
- `StandardSchemaV1`

Source: `packages/core/types/`

---
